transcript: This is one of the filthiest things I've ever seen, and I don't even really know how to describe it. So this was from the Euro Hockey Tour, and if you're one of my Czech or Finnish or Sweden viewers, you're, I'm sure, familiar with this, but for North Americans, it's basically a yearly tournament comprised of those three teams plus Russia, which has right now been replaced by Switzerland. This was a game between Finland and Sweden, obviously, which Sweden won 4-3 in the shootout. Anyway, the shot we're discussing here was by Timu Hardikainen. Oilers fans may remember him. He was a prospect for a couple of years and played about 50 games in the NHL. But as for the shot itself, I don't even know how to describe this. I mean, I'm not even sure I understand what's happening, but it seems like it is a full one-handed toe drag, flips the stick onto his face, and then essentially snaps it up. Now, he he did miss the net and i don't even think had he scored it would have counted based on international shootout rules but i mean the flexibility strength and control you need to even attempt this is insane if i tried to do this standing on land with a floor hockey ball i would probably fully fully dislocate my shoulder. And it's like a modified wrist shot as well. The way he shifts his stick and then essentially snaps it at the net. And like, it's impressive when you watch it in slow motion, but at full speed, the fact that he was even able to get the puck on net is incredibly impressive. Definitely one of the more creative shootout attempts I've seen, but let me know what you think down below.
